reference.domains: (topic reference) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c reference oceReference-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
reference.title: Sales/Service Organization IDentifier (SOID)

abstract.p: To enable population management per service organization, we need a method to determine each service organization uniquely. By doing so, the service organization can only see and control their own machines in the service portals.
An identifier (SOID) has been implemented. This identifier is unique for each service organization and can be created and obtained via the service master data portal.

We use the SOID for the direct organization and also for the indirect organizations, like our partners. The organization that sells the machines is considered the owner of the service contract. It can decide to do the service by itself or outsource the service to another Service Organization. The SOID is implemented as a hierarchy. The visibility of machines is limited to your own organization and the underlying organizations.

Once the SOID is known, it must be entered into the print system. This can be done for new machines during installation in the installation wizard, or in the settings of the engine for machines already in the field. Having the SOID filled is a prerequisite to properly work with On Remote Service (ORS) portal.

A check mechanism has been implemented to make sure that only valid entries are made. A verification code must be entered that matches the SOID. This verification code is calculated from the entered SOID. No online connection to a remote server is needed. Only correct matches between SOID and verification code are accepted.

section.title: Service Masterdata Portal
section.p: The Service MasterData Portal is available for authorized users via https://servicemd.cpp.canon
Under the tab 'Service Organization' the setup of all service organizations is shown.
The SOID is implemented as a hierarchy. The visibility of machines is limited to your own organization and the underlying organizations.
When selecting a specific service organization, the details for that organization are shown. This includes the SOID and the verification code.

section.title: How to become authorized
section.p: Authorization is controlled top down. HQ authorizes RSHQ, RSHQ authorizes NSO and regional dealers, and NSO authorizes local dealers.

section.title: Usage of the SOID
section.p: There are several configuration aspects. An organization that (partly) outsources the service activities must configure who their outsourcing parties are. These are called Servicing Partners. Per machine can be indicated with which servicing partner the service is shared.

section.title: Configuration on organization level
section.p: An organization that outsources machine service, needs to configure its servicing partners.
Configuring is done in the PRISMAservice portal ( https://prismaservice.cpp.canon ). Multiple Servicing partners can be configured.
Login on the PRISMAservice portal,

li.p: Select the '+' sign to add a servicing partner.
A popup is shown where you need to fill out the SOID code and the matching verification code.

section.p: The SOID of the Canon organizations can be found in the TSM.
See: What to do when you do not know the SOID

section.title: Configuration at engine level
section.p: When installing an engine, during the installation wizard is requested to enter the SOID.
The SOID of the organization that sold the machine needs to be filled out.
See: How to enter the Sales/Service Organization ID (SOID) on the printer
Sharing service of a machine is done per machine, as every machine can have a different servicing organization e.g. due to geographical reasons.
A machine that has its service being outsourced, needs to be shared explicitly with the organization it is outsource to.
By doing so, the machine can also be taken along in the service figures of the outsourced organization.

Depending on the hierarchy it can be that the machine is already visible. Report filtering requires the Sharing of an individual machine. Sharing an individual machine is done in ORS ( https://prismaservice.cpp.canon ) .
Login on the PRISMAservice portal.

li.p: Open the product selector app, search for the applicable engine and double-click the entry to open the product app for this machine.
Go to the menu Collect printer info > Service partners.
Click the pencil icon adjacent to the field Service delegated to.

note.p: If the pencil icon is unavailable, you do not have sufficient access rights (required is the site admin role).

li.p: From the pull down menu, Service delegated to, select the service partner by whom the machine will be serviced.

p.b: Correct the SOID ( only for temporary correction)

section.p: When the SOID was configured incorrectly it needs to be corrected at the machine. It is possible to override the SOID of the machine in the ORS settings of the machine.
This must be temporary and changed at next visit. Rationale is that information retrieved locally at the engine the SOID is set incorrectly.
Login on the PRISMAservice portal.

li.p: Open the product selector app, search for the applicable engine and double-click the entry to open the product app for this machine.
Go to the menu Collect printer info > General.
Click the pencil icon adjacent to the field Service organization.
From the pull down menu, New, select the name of the service organization that will execute the service.

section.title: What to do when SOID is not known
section.p: In the installation wizard it is mandatory to fill in the SOID. If the correct SOID is not known, it is advised to enter the RSHQ or HQ SOID.
See: What to do when you do not know the SOID
